content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Hal Stocker 
Yuba County Board of Supervisors 
P O  Box 366 
Challenge, CA 95925 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-051 
Dear Mr. Stocker: 
April 12, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign contribution 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1 regulating the proper use of campaign funds. 
QUESTION 
May you donate unspent funds from your last supervisorial campaign to public schools in 
Yuba County? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The donation may be made to Yuba County schools so long as the proceeds will not 
have a material financial effect on you, a member of your immediate family, your campaign 
treasurer, or any individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign 
funds held by your committee. 
FACTS 
You were reelected to the Yuba County Board of Supervisors in June 2010. You have 
unexpended campaign funds from this campaign. You wish to donate the campaign funds to 
Yuba County public schools. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 lhrough 91014. All slatutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are conlained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


ANALYSIS 
File No. A-11-051 
Page No. 2 
Section 89519 applies very specific rules on the use of surplus campaign funds in a 
candidate's campaign committee after he or she loses an election or leaves office. However, 
Section 89519 only applies to incumbents upon leaving an elected office, or at the end of the 
postelection reporting period following the def eat of a candidate for elective office, whichever 
occurs last. Because you were re-elected to the Yuba County Board of Supervisors in June 2010, 
this section would not apply to your use of funds. 
Rather, your use of campaign funds is governed by Sections 89510-89518. Under the 
general provisions governing the use of campaign funds, a candidate may only use campaign 
funds if the expenditure bears either a reasonable or a direct relationship to a political, legislative 
or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.) Where an expenditure of campaign funds confers a 
substantial personal benefit2 on a candidate, however, the expenditure must be directly related to 
a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.) 
Donation of the Funds 
"Campaign funds may be used to make donations or loans to bona fide charitable, 
educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit organizations, where 
no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the 
candidate, elected officer, campaign treasurer, or any individual or individuals with 
authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a committee, or 
member of his or her immediate family, and where the donation or loan bears a 
reasonable relation to a political, legislative or governmental purpose" (Section 
89515.) [Emphasis added.] 
We have previously defined a "material financial effect" as used in this section as an 
effect of $250 or more. ( O'Neil Advice letter, No. A-98-268(a).) 
Your facts indicate that you will be making a donation to public schools in Yuba County, 
which are bona fide educational institutions. Thus it appears that the donation would bear a 
reasonable relation to a governmental purpose. Therefore, the donation may be made to Yuba 
County schools so long as the proceeds will not have a material financial effect on you, a 
member of your immediate family, your campaign treasurer, or any individual or individuals 
with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by your committee. 
2 ·•substantial personal benefit" means an expenditure of campaign funds that results in a direct personal 
benefit with a value to the candidate of more than $200. (Section 895 I l(b)(3).) 


ER:jgl 
File No. A-11-051 
Page No. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
John W. Wallace 
Assistant General Counsel 
h 
Counsel, Legal Division
